result:
I've completed the read-only inspection of all three focus files plus targeted greps. Here is my review.

## C-MPACK-02 — Requirement extraction completeness review

**Overall verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** (p0: 0, p1: 0, p2: 0, p3: 1)

### What I verified (read-only)

**1. Family extraction — 12 active families present, 4 declared-empty, M365 delegated.**
A full enumeration of row IDs (candidates file lines 29–228) shows exactly **200 candidate rows** across: CORE, PERM, ISSUE, PORTAL, VAULT, AIGW, DRAFT, BILL, HR, UI, API, GOV. Per-family row totals sum cleanly to 200 (CORE 18, PERM 40, ISSUE 33, PORTAL 4, VAULT 9, AIGW 9, DRAFT 6, BILL 9, HR 16, UI 14, API 31, GOV 11). This matches `Candidate rows: 200` (line 232) and the matrix metric `requirement_candidate_rows | 200` (matrix line 11).

**2. Named-object counts reconcile across both focus docs and the actual rows.** The matrix "Must Not Miss" table (lines 19–26) and the candidates "Counts" block (lines 232–243) agree with the rows I counted:

| Group | Claimed | Verified rows |
|---|---|---|
| Domain objects | 17 | CORE-101..117 ✓ |
| Roles | 12 | PERM-101..112 ✓ |
| Audit event classes | 12 | PERM-213..224 ✓ |
| Permission eval steps | 10 | PERM-325..334 ✓ |
| Ethical Wall + override | 6 | PERM-435..439 + 499 ✓ |
| Practice modules | 32 | ISSUE-158..189 (12 M&A + 10 Litigation + 10 Corporate) ✓ |
| Issue Ledger invariant fields | 16 | ISSUE-301 enumerates 16 ✓ |
| HR objects | 16 | HR-101..116 ✓ |
| UI screens | 14 | UI-101..114 ✓ |
| API endpoints + events | 31 | API-101..116 (16) + API-201..215 (15) ✓ |

**3. M365 exclusion from the master candidate ledger — confirmed.** `M365` appears only in the exclusion note (line 3) and the `M365 family rows in this file: 0` count (line 244); zero M365 rows in the ledger. M365's appearance in the matrix checksum (11 `defer_with_revisit_gate`) documents the delegation rather than silently dropping it — correct, and not a ledger violation.

**4. Forbidden standalone terms Activity / Lead / Contact — none present.** Case-sensitive word-boundary greps for `Activity`, `Lead`, `Contact` returned **no matches**. Case-insensitive sweep shows only the approved canonical compounds: `activity_signal` (BILL-224), `matter_stage_lead` (CORE-301 terminology_ref), and `matter_participant_role` (CORE-107). The single `lead` hit at line 106 is the substring inside "P**lead**ing Tracker" (ISSUE-177), not the forbidden term. `Contact` is entirely absent — consistent with the terminology map's rule (line 15) to convert Plan A "Contact" into the `matter_participant_role` concept rather than create a Contact object.

Planning-only discipline holds: every row carries `units_currently_added: 0`; no production_ready/contract/validator claim in any focus file.

### Finding (P3)

- **[P3] WORK / ADMIN / INTEG / HARD completeness cannot be positively confirmed from the focus files.** These four families (named in the review scope) carry zero candidate rows and zero section_count in the matrix checksum (lines 60–66, 109–129). They are *declared* empty (so not a silent omission), but — unlike M365 — no explicit justification appears in the three focus files. Positive confirmation that the source workbook genuinely has no WORK/ADMIN/INTEG/HARD headings depends on `matter-pack-source-index.md` / the workbook, which is **outside the provided focus set**. Recommend confirming against the source index before treating these families as exhaustively extracted.

### Notes / limitations

- The "Family x Classification Checksum" `section_count` column sums to **201**, matching `source_headings_classified_once | 201` (matrix line 10) — i.e., it tallies *source headings*, not the 200 candidate rows, and uses the richer 7-value classification taxonomy. It is internally consistent on that basis; I did not flag it as a row-count contradiction. Full verification of these per-bucket heading counts requires the source index (out of scope).
- The 201 headings → 200 rows delta is consistent with one deliberate non-absorption (the "Loop" methodology term, terminology map line 16: "요구로 추출하지 않음"), though I could not confirm this against the source index.
- Scope honored: M365 extraction completeness was not assessed (delegated to C-MPACK-M365-01).

Everything the packet explicitly required — named-object counts, M365 ledger exclusion, and the Activity/Lead/Contact prohibition — passes cleanly; the only residual is the P3 inability to positively confirm the four empty families without the source index. Ready to produce the JSON-only restatement on request.
